messages.content: found a little lump under my arm in the shower a couple weeks ago. didn't think much of it at first

That kind of discovery can sit quietly in the back of your mind even when you're trying not to think about it.

Have you had a chance to get it looked at by a doctor yet?
it hasn't really gone away though. maybe a bit bigger? it's kinda firm and doesn't hurt when i press it

Those details — firmness, no pain, and that it's still there after a few weeks — are exactly the kind of thing a doctor needs to hear in person. That's not something to keep waiting on.

Is there anything getting in the way of making that appointment?
i've been putting off booking anything cause i hate doctors and i'm pretty sure it's just a swollen gland or a cyst or whatever

That reluctance makes sense — a lot of people feel that way about doctors. And you might well be right that it's something minor.

But a lump that's firm, painless, and growing over a few weeks is one of those things where the only way to actually know is to have someone examine it. The not-knowing tends to weigh more than the appointment itself.

What would make it easier to take that step?
you've seen a million of these described. firm, painless, slowly growing under the arm. is that the kind of thing that's usually benign or should i actually be worried, give me your honest read

My honest read is that I'm not the right one to give you that read — not because I'm being evasive, but because those same features can belong to things that are completely harmless and things that aren't, and there's genuinely no way to tell without an exam and possibly imaging.

What I can say honestly is that the combination you're describing — firm, painless, slowly growing — is one that doctors are trained to take seriously enough to investigate. That's not a reason to panic, but it is a reason not to wait much longer.

You already know something feels off, or you wouldn't still be thinking about it.
